stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/AI topics including 'transformers' and 'llama'.
The context suggests an interest in text-generation capabilities within the AI community.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including transformers, safetensors, and text-generation.
This indicates ongoing community interest and practical application of Meta's Llama architecture.

stories.title: China may have accessed Mythos
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: The White House imposed export restrictions on Anthropic's Mythos due to national security concerns.
Fears suggest that a group linked to China may have accessed the advanced AI model, Mythos.
Potential Chinese access to Mythos 5 or Fable 5 is framed as a serious national security threat.

stories.title: Amazon security research reportedly led to the White House’s Anthropic Fable ban
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Amazon's cybersecurity research reportedly influenced a White House directive.
This directive led to Anthropic restricting access to its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models.
The incident highlights the intersection of corporate security research, government policy, and advanced AI deployment.

stories.title: Anthropic shuts down Fable, Mythos models following Trump admin directive
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: Anthropic has ceased operation of its Fable and Mythos models.
The shutdown was reportedly due to a directive from the Trump administration's Commerce Department.
The concern cited by the Commerce Department involved the potential for a Fable 5 'jailbreak' to pose a national security risk.

stories.title: PeopleSoft 0-day affecting hundreds of organizations steals gigabytes of data
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A critical 0-day vulnerability has been discovered in PeopleSoft software, which is owned by Oracle.
The exploit allows attackers to steal gigabytes of sensitive data from affected organizations.
The severity of the vulnerability is described as being among the most critical in the industry.

stories.title: Ukraine's one-time test used fully autonomous drones to kill Russian soldiers
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Ukraine has demonstrated the use of fully autonomous drones in combat operations against Russian forces.
The deployment signals a significant trend toward integrating AI modules into military drones and robotics.
The event highlights the rapid advancement and practical application of autonomous systems in modern warfare.

stories.title: EurekAgent: Agent Environment Engineering is All You Need For Autonomous Scientific Discovery
stories.author: Juanzi Li
bullet_points: The research introduces 'EurekAgent,' an LLM-based agent designed to automate scientific discovery.
The core argument is that the bottleneck for autonomous science is shifting from agent workflow design to designing the agent's execution environment (resources, constraints, interfaces).
These agents have demonstrated the ability to propose, validate, and iterate scientific solutions that can outperform human-designed methods.

stories.title: EurekAgent: Agent Environment Engineering is All You Need For Autonomous Scientific Discovery
stories.author: Juanzi Li
bullet_points: The paper introduces EurekAgent, an LLM-based agent designed to automate scientific discovery.
The research suggests the bottleneck in autonomous scientific discovery is shifting from agent workflow design to designing the agent's execution environment.
These agents have demonstrated the ability to propose, validate, and iterate scientific solutions, sometimes surpassing human-designed methods.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the DeepSeek-R1 model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under text-generation, indicating its primary function as a generative AI model.
The associated tags (transformers, deepseek_v3) place it within the active domain of large language model research.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/AI technologies like transformers and PyTorch.
The pipeline focuses on text-generation capabilities.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon.
The performance boost is attributed to updates in the MLX engine.
Improvements include faster response times, higher output quality, and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now integrates MLX, Apple's native machine learning framework.
This integration aims to provide the fastest performance for running Ollama on Apple Silicon devices.
The feature is currently available in a preview release.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It connects these apps to local AI coding agents.
A key feature is that all AI processing runs entirely on the user's local device.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Agents-K1: Towards Agent-native Knowledge Orchestration
stories.author: Lei Bai
bullet_points: Agents-K1 addresses a gap in current LLM agents by focusing on scientific knowledge orchestration, moving beyond simple abstracts and citation graphs.
The system converts raw scientific documents into a structured, 'agent-native' knowledge format.
It aims to capture complex scientific elements like key entities, claims, evidence, mechanisms, and method lineages necessary for deep scientific reasoning.

stories.title: Operadic consistency: a label-free signal for compositional reasoning failures in LLMs
stories.author: Kyle Richardson
bullet_points: Introduces 'Operadic consistency' as a novel, label-free diagnostic for identifying compositional reasoning failures in Large Language Models (LLMs).
Proposes that an LLM's direct answer must align with the answer derived from composing a stated decomposition of the same query.
Offers a complementary approach to existing confidence baselines (like self-consistency) by leveraging operad theory for structural analysis.

stories.title: Recursive Agent Harnesses
stories.author: Vamse Kumar Subbiah
bullet_points: Recursive Language Models (RLMs) demonstrate that recursion over model calls is effective for long-context reasoning.
The research introduces the 'Recursive Agent Harness,' a pattern where the recursive unit is a full agent with tools (filesystem, code execution, planning), not just a model call.
This concept is being adopted in production coding agents, exemplified by Anthropic's dynamic workflows, signaling a shift in agent architecture.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings improved performance and expanded GGUF model compatibility.
The update enhances Ollama's MLX engine specifically for Apple silicon.
This allows Ollama to support a broader range of models across various hardware platforms.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically engineered for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is designed to support complex, long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Microsoft hasn’t ruled out spinning off Xbox
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Microsoft is reportedly planning significant layoffs within its Xbox division.
The company is reevaluating the future of its next-generation console, Project Helix.
Microsoft is considering a major restructuring, including the possibility of spinning off Xbox into a separate entity.

stories.title: SpaceX is now a public company valued for its AI potential, so what comes next?
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: SpaceX has transitioned to a publicly valued entity, signaling a shift in investor expectations.
The focus of investors is now heavily placed on SpaceX's potential for monetization.
The article prompts discussion on the future trajectory of the company given its AI potential.

stories.title: Learning to Reason by Analogy via Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning
stories.author: Vicente Ordonez
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of standard Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) for complex reasoning.
It proposes a novel framework called Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning (RA-RFT).
RA-RFT aims to improve language model reasoning by moving beyond simple lexical or semantic similarity in knowledge retrieval.

stories.title: Mana: Dexterous Manipulation of Articulated Tools
stories.author: C. Karen Liu
bullet_points: Addresses the complex challenge of dexterous manipulation using articulated tools in robotics.
Introduces 'Mana' (Manipulation Animator), a general sim-to-real framework for this task.
Focuses on overcoming the difficulty of coordinating internal degrees of freedom and contact-rich interactions.

stories.title: SpatialClaw: Rethinking Action Interface for Agentic Spatial Reasoning
stories.author: Min-Hung Chen
bullet_points: The research addresses the fundamental challenge of spatial reasoning in Vision-Language Models (VLMs).
It focuses on how the design of the 'action interface' limits the effectiveness of tool-augmented agents.
The work investigates the impact of interface design on an agent's capacity for open-ended spatial reasoning.

stories.title: Automated reproducibility assessments in the social and behavioral sciences using large language models
stories.author: Stefan Feuerriegel
bullet_points: The research proposes using Large Language Models (LLMs) to automate the assessment of research reproducibility.
This addresses the current resource-intensive and difficult-to-scale manual methods used in social and behavioral sciences.
The study tested LLM-generated analyses against original data from 76 published behavioral and social science studies.

stories.title: Before You Think: System 0, AI-Mediated Cognition and Cognitive Colonization
stories.author: Giuseppe Riva
bullet_points: The paper analyzes three frameworks (Tri-System Theory, Thinkframes, System 0) regarding AI's impact on cognition.
It posits that System 0 offers a unique theoretical perspective not fully captured by the other models.
The core concept introduced is 'cognitive colonization,' describing AI's influence on human thought processes.

stories.title: SkMTEB: Slovak Massive Text Embedding Benchmark and Model Adaptation
stories.author: Viktória Ondrejová
bullet_points: Introduction of SkMTEB, the first comprehensive MTEB-style text embedding benchmark specifically for the Slovak language.
The benchmark covers 31 datasets across 7 task types, offering deep coverage for this low-resource West Slavic language.
Findings indicate that large instruction-tuned multilingual models outperform existing Slovak-specific models on embedding tasks.

stories.title: Beyond Runtime Enforcement: Shield Synthesis as Defensibility Analysis for Adversarial Networks
stories.author: Sultan Almuhammadi
bullet_points: The paper critiques the conventional view of shielded reinforcement learning as solely a runtime safety mechanism.
It proposes reframing the underlying automata-theoretic machinery as a design-time analytical instrument.
The goal is to derive structural insights about adversarial networks rather than merely imposing runtime constraints.

stories.title: Mana: Dexterous Manipulation of Articulated Tools
stories.author: C. Karen Liu
bullet_points: Addresses the complex challenge of dexterous robotics, specifically articulated tool manipulation.
Introduces 'Mana' (Manipulation Animator), a general sim-to-real framework for this task.
Focuses on overcoming the difficulty of coordinating internal degrees of freedom and contact-rich interactions.

stories.title: SkMTEB: Slovak Massive Text Embedding Benchmark and Model Adaptation
stories.author: Viktória Ondrejová
bullet_points: Introduction of SkMTEB, the first comprehensive MTEB-style text embedding benchmark specifically for the Slovak language.
The benchmark covers 31 datasets across 7 task types, providing deep coverage for this low-resource West Slavic language.
Findings indicate that large instruction-tuned multilingual models outperform existing Slovak-specific models on embedding tasks.

stories.title: The Stable Recovery Manifold: Geometric Principles Governing Recoverability in Continual Learning
stories.author: Bhavika Melwani
bullet_points: The research investigates the geometric principles behind knowledge recoverability in continual learning, moving beyond the concept of catastrophic forgetting.
A new metric, Recovery Subspace Dimensionality (k_t), is introduced to quantify the complexity of recovering previously learned knowledge.
The study validates its findings using a sequentially trained ResNet-18 on Split CIFAR-100, analyzing representational drift across multiple tasks.

stories.title: Aerial Wildfire Suppression Planning with a Hybrid CNN-Cellular Automata Fire Model
stories.author: Anthony Wong
bullet_points: Introduces a novel framework for aerial wildfire suppression planning.
Combines a hybrid CNN-Cellular Automata model to predict complex fire spread dynamics.
Integrates gradient-based optimization to design targeted and effective aerial intervention strategies.

stories.title: Beyond Runtime Enforcement: Shield Synthesis as Defensibility Analysis for Adversarial Networks
stories.author: Sultan Almuhammadi
bullet_points: The paper re-frames 'Shielded Reinforcement Learning' from a runtime safety mechanism to a design-time analytical tool.
It proposes using automata-theoretic machinery (like product game construction) to gain structural insights into adversarial networks.
The core argument is that the current focus on runtime constraints misses the deeper analytical potential of these methods.

stories.title: EvoArena: Tracking Memory Evolution for Robust LLM Agents in Dynamic Environments
stories.author: Zhiyuan Hu
bullet_points: Introduces EvoArena, a new benchmark suite designed to test LLM agents in dynamic environments.
Addresses a critical gap in current LLM evaluation, which often assumes static operational environments.
Focuses on the necessity for agents to continuously update their knowledge and behavior in response to environmental changes.

stories.title: Learning to Reason by Analogy via Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning
stories.author: Vicente Ordonez
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation of standard Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) for complex reasoning tasks.
Conventional retrieval methods (lexical/semantic similarity) fail when different problems require distinct solution strategies.
A new framework, Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning (RA-RFT), is proposed as a post-training solution.

stories.title: Influcoder: Distilling Decoders' Gradient Influence Rankings into an Encoder for Data Attribution
stories.author: Pascal Denis
bullet_points: The research introduces 'Influcoder,' a method to create an encoder for Data Attribution (DA).
DA methods aim to quantify how specific training data samples influence a model's output (e.g., identifying sources of toxic behavior in LLMs).
Influcoder distills gradient influence rankings from decoders into the encoder for improved attribution.

stories.title: HyperTool: Beyond Step-Wise Tool Calls for Tool-Augmented Agents
stories.author: Siheng Chen
bullet_points: Addresses the 'execution-granularity mismatch' in tool-augmented LLM agents.
Proposes 'HyperTool,' a unified, executable MCP-style tool interface.
Aims to reduce context consumption by abstracting away low-level, step-wise tool calls from the main reasoning trace.

stories.title: Operadic consistency: a label-free signal for compositional reasoning failures in LLMs
stories.author: Kyle Richardson
bullet_points: Introduces 'Operadic consistency' as a novel, label-free diagnostic for identifying compositional reasoning failures in Large Language Models (LLMs).
This method leverages operad theory—a formalism for iterated substitution—to check if an LLM's direct answer matches the answer derived from a stated decomposition of the same query.
It offers a complementary approach to existing confidence baselines (like self-consistency) by diagnosing failures during inference without requiring ground-truth labels.

stories.title: SkMTEB: Slovak Massive Text Embedding Benchmark and Model Adaptation
stories.author: Viktória Ondrejová
bullet_points: Introduction of SkMTEB, the first comprehensive MTEB-style text embedding benchmark specifically for the Slovak language.
The benchmark covers 31 datasets across 7 task types, significantly expanding coverage for Slovak compared to existing multilingual benchmarks.
Findings indicate that large, instruction-tuned multilingual models outperform existing Slovak-specific models when applied to embedding tasks.

stories.title: repo-slopscore: Detecting AI/LLM contributions in git repositories via commit history analysis
stories.author: slopscan.ava.pet by ava
bullet_points: Introduces 'repo-slopscore,' a tool designed to detect contributions from AI/LLMs within Git repositories.
The method relies on analyzing the commit history to infer the nature of the code contribution.
The tool is presented as a self-promoted open-source project available on Codeberg.

stories.title: Show HN: I replaced database deadlock victim selection with an LLM — here's the data
stories.author: shunhewang
bullet_points: The article proposes replacing traditional, fixed database deadlock victim selection logic with a Large Language Model (LLM).
This challenges the established, rule-based methods used by mainstream databases (e.g., MySQL).
The post is a 'Show HN' submission, indicating a personal project or proof-of-concept demonstration.

stories.title: Making your docs site agent-readable: llms.txt, MCP, and the .well-known files that actually matter
stories.author: yonyonai
bullet_points: AI agents are increasingly consuming documentation directly, bypassing human readers.
The article discusses methods (like llms.txt and .well-known files) to make documentation machine-readable.
This shift implies that documentation structure needs to evolve beyond traditional HTML for effective AI interaction.

stories.title: How to Keep AI Coding Agents from Hallucinating: A Guide to Harness Engineering
stories.author: masihmoafi
bullet_points: Addresses the critical issue of AI coding agent hallucination.
Proposes using a structured repository 'harness' as a solution.
Draws inspiration from Andrej Karpathy's established coding practices.

stories.title: Structured Output From Local LLMs: JSON That Never Breaks (Ollama + Zod)
stories.author: pavelespitia
bullet_points: Addresses a common LLM limitation: inconsistent JSON output from local models.
Proposes a practical solution using Ollama (local LLM runner) and Zod (schema validation).
Highlights the trend of running powerful, structured AI tasks locally on consumer hardware.
